title: "How to disable Touchpad for MSI S270?"

forum: Hardware &amp; Laptops

### Post by Spippo on 2007-07-18
Hi all,

I have a MSI S270 laptop with Ubuntu64. All is working well, but I can't turn off my touchpad. (No, I don't have a little button to turn it off). I searched for this problem for a long time, and found several solutions, but none of them worked. Can anybody help me?

Here is de content of my **_/etc/X11/xorg.conf_**:
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.

Section "Files"

        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc105"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "be"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"      "ImPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"  "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"

       Option          "Type"  "stylus"
       Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

       Identifier      "eraser"
       Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
       Option          "Type"  "eraser"
       Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "ATI Technologies Inc ATI Radeon XPRESS 200M 5955 (PCIE)"
        Driver          "fglrx"
        Busid           "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"

        Monitor         "Generic Monitor"
        Defaultdepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth   1
                Modes           "1024x768"      "800x600"       "640x480"

                Modes           "1024x768"      "800x600"       "640x480"

        Mode    0666
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
        Option          "Composite"     "0"
EndSection

```

Also, if I try the the *synclient -l* command, I always get the following error:
***Can't access shared memory area. SHMConfig disabled?***

Thanks

Tim

---

### Post by Death_Sargent on 2007-07-18
i have a way to fix that just give me alitle under an hour

---

### Post by Death_Sargent on 2007-07-18
ok here it goes for this  section 

```
Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"      "ImPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"  "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection
```

add this

```
Option	"SHMConfig"	"true"
```

then run this command 

```
sudo apt-get install gsynaptics
```

you can change the setting from that program

it is found under System>Preferences>Touchpad

---

I also changed the *true* into *on* (as found in other threads). And I restarted X after each try.

### Post by Death_Sargent on 2007-07-22
try this

```
Option "MaxTapTime" "0"
```

in your "Configured mouse" section of your xorg.conf

### Post by Ayuthia on 2007-07-23
You might try at the terminal:
synclient TouchpadOff=1

Here is the link that contains this information.  If this doesn't work, you might check in your bios to see if you can turn off the touchpad in there.

[http://ubuntu.wordpress.com/2006/03/24/disable-synaptics-touchpad/](http://ubuntu.wordpress.com/2006/03/24/disable-synaptics-touchpad/)

---

### Post by Spippo on 2007-07-23
That gives me the same error of **Can't access shared memory area. SHMConfig disabled?**.
I also found the link you gave me, that didn't helped me.
All the topics i find on the net, they all have a seperate section of their touchpad, i don't. Maybe that's the problem?

I can't turn off the touchpad in bios.

---

### Post by Ayuthia on 2007-07-23
Here is another link that might match up with your problem.  The solution is to take out the mouse module by using rmmod.  

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=328851&page=2](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=328851&page=2)

From what I have found so far in my searches is that you might have an Elantech touchpad which is not compatible with synaptics which explains why you are not able to get the synaptics stuff to work.

---

### Post by Ayuthia on 2007-07-23
You might also try using:
Option “SendCoreEvents” “true”
in your original xorg.conf file.  I think that is supposed to stop the mouse from sending information.  However, I am not for sure if it will stop your external mouse (or other mouse device) or not.

---

### Post by Spippo on 2007-07-24
Thank you very much!!!!!!!!!!!!

With just 1 simple command, my touchpad is disabled and my exernal mouse still works.
the **sudo rmmod psmouse** works great!!!

On the website of MSI, they say I have a synaptics touchpad. Also in Windows preferences there also is synaptics

---

### Post by Ayuthia on 2007-07-24
I am glad that it worked!  Here is the link that led me to believe that it does not work with Synaptics:

[http://forum.notebookreview.com/archive/index.php?t-46919.html](http://forum.notebookreview.com/archive/index.php?t-46919.html)

From what that page said, it sounded like the Synaptics driver in Windows had some limited functionality.

---
